channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Geopolitical news headlines from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🏛️ Supreme Court Hollows Out Voting Rights Act After 61 Years
item.description: The U.S. Supreme Court knocked out a major pillar of the Voting Rights Act, eliminating protections against racial discrimination in voting and redistricting that had stood for six decades. Civil rights advocates warn the decision could return minority communities to the Jim Crow era.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran Supreme Leader Vows to Protect Nuclear and Missile Capabilities
item.description: Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei declared Iran will defend its nuclear and missile programs as national assets, drawing a hard line in ceasefire negotiations with the US. His statement comes as Brent crude surged past $126 per barrel amid the continued Strait of Hormuz closure.

item.title: 🛢️ Brent Crude Surges Past $126 as Iran War Blocks Hormuz Strait
item.description: Oil prices hit a four-year high with Brent crude briefly exceeding $126 per barrel as stalled US-Iran negotiations kept the Strait of Hormuz closed. Prices have nearly doubled from $70 before the war began in late February.

item.title: 💰 US-Iran War Has Cost $25 Billion So Far, Pentagon Official Says
item.description: A Pentagon official provided the first official cost estimate for the US military campaign against Iran, revealing the conflict has already consumed $25 billion as the war enters its third month.

item.title: 🇱🇧 Over 1 Million in Lebanon Face Acute Food Insecurity Crisis
item.description: A global hunger monitor warned that more than 1 million people in Lebanon will face acute food insecurity in coming months due to renewed conflict and mass displacement, compounding the humanitarian crisis in the region.

item.title: 🇬🇧 UK Declares Antisemitism Emergency After Double Stabbing Attack
item.description: Two Jewish men were stabbed in north London by an attacker targeting Jews. Police arrested a suspect and are treating the attack as terrorism. UK Security Minister pledged new legislation to tackle state-sponsored threats.

item.title: 🇻🇪 Venezuela Resumes Commercial Flights to US After Decades
item.description: Venezuela and the US reached an agreement to resume commercial flights between Caracas and US cities for the first time in decades, marking a potential thaw in relations between the two nations.

item.title: 📰 Media Organizations Demand Gaza Access for Foreign Journalists
item.description: Leaders of major media organizations worldwide called on Israel to lift its ban preventing foreign journalists from independently entering Gaza, citing press freedom and public's right to know.

item.title: 🏦 Fed's Powell Reflects on Era of Economic Challenges
item.description: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell looked back on a tumultuous period of economic challenges, from pandemic recovery to inflation battles and now geopolitical shocks from the Iran war.

item.title: ⚔️ Ukraine Commander Orders Mandatory Front-Line Rotations
item.description: Ukraine's military chief Syrskyi ordered mandatory rotations for soldiers after two months at the front lines, addressing troop fatigue in the ongoing war with Russia.

item.title: ✈️ Middle East Airlines See 60% Drop in Demand Amid War
item.description: Regional carriers experienced a dramatic decline in passenger demand during March as the Iran war disrupted travel patterns and deterred tourism across the Middle East.

item.title: 🇵🇰 Pakistan Opens 6 Land Routes to Iran Amid Hormuz Blockade
item.description: Pakistan announced six new land routes to Iran, providing a strategic lifeline that risks diplomatic friction with the US as Washington seeks to isolate Tehran economically.

item.title: 💰 Wall Street Giants Circle Shell's $15 Billion LNG Canada Stake
item.description: Major financial institutions are positioning to acquire Shell's stake in the LNG Canada project, valued at approximately $15 billion, as global energy markets restructure.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Hezbollah Deploys Fiber-Optic Drones Against Israel
item.description: Hezbollah is using a new type of drone controlled by fiber-optic cables, similar to those deployed in Ukraine, presenting Israel with a novel battlefield challenge.

item.title: 🏛️ Supreme Court Weakens Voting Rights in Redistricting Case
item.description: The US Supreme Court ruled against civil rights groups challenging Louisiana's congressional maps, dealing another blow to minority voting protections established under the Voting Rights Act.

item.title: 💊 Mexico Sinaloa Cartel Drug Trafficking Operations Exposed
item.description: New details emerged about Mexican drug cartel operations as authorities continued investigations into the Sinaloa cartel's trafficking networks and methods.

item.title: 💶 ECB Holds Rates Steady, Keeps Future Hikes on Table
item.description: The European Central Bank kept interest rates unchanged while signaling that further rate increases remain possible, maintaining a cautious stance amid persistent inflation pressures.

item.title: 🛢️ Oil Prices Extend Gains as US-Iran War Deadlock Keeps Supply Off Market
item.description: Crude oil prices continued climbing as the ongoing conflict between the US and Iran kept significant supplies from reaching global markets, with no resolution in sight.

item.title: 🇨🇳 Taiwan Tops Beijing's Agenda for Trump-Xi Summit
item.description: Taiwan remains China's top priority in anticipated discussions between President Trump and Xi Jinping, as Beijing continues pressing its claims over the self-governing island.

item.title: 📊 Fed Holds Rates Steady in Most Divided Vote Since 1992
item.description: The Federal Reserve kept interest rates unchanged with the most split board vote in over three decades, revealing deep internal divisions over monetary policy direction.

item.title: 🇨🇳 Chinese Experts Map How Iran Damaged US F-35 Stealth Jet
item.description: Chinese military analysts published assessments of how Iranian forces detected and damaged a US F-35 stealth fighter, drawing lessons about advanced aircraft vulnerabilities.

item.title: 🇧🇾 Belarus Blocks Conscripts From Leaving for Russia and Occupied Ukraine
item.description: Belarus prohibited conscripts from departing to Russia and Russian-occupied Ukrainian territories, signaling Minsk's complex position in the ongoing regional conflict.

item.title: 🛢️ Iran War Exposes Big Oil's Transatlantic Divide
item.description: The conflict between the US and Iran has revealed significant differences between American and European energy companies in how they're navigating sanctions and supply disruptions.
